title: ceratostylis subcoerulea var. subcoerulea
intro: Ceratostylis subcoerulea var. subcoerulea, also known as the blue orchid, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Ceratostylis subcoerulea var. subcoerulea include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are typically harvested and used to make teas, infusions, or tonics.
